intro: Passiflora myrsinites, also known as Passionflower or Maypop, is a perennial vine native to the southeastern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Passifloraceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative properties, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The bioactive constituents of Passiflora myrsinites include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, such as harman, harmine, and harmaline, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
